name: microsoft-kiota-http

metadata.name: microsoft-kiota-http
metadata.summary: Core abstractions for kiota generated libraries in Python

documentation:
# Microsoft Kiota HTTP library

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/microsoft-kiota-http.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/microsoft-kiota-http)
[![Downloads](https://pepy.tech/badge/microsoft-kiota-http)](https://pepy.tech/project/microsoft-kiota-http)

The Microsoft Kiota HTTP Library is a python HTTP implementation with HTTPX library.

A [Kiota](https://github.com/microsoft/kiota) generated project will need a reference to a http package to to make HTTP requests to an API endpoint.

Read more about Kiota [here](https://github.com/microsoft/kiota/blob/main/README.md).

## Using the Microsoft Kiota HTTP library

In order to use this library, install the package by running:

```cmd
pip install microsoft-kiota-http
```

## Contributing

This project welcomes contributions and suggestions.  Most contributions require you to agree to a
Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us
the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit <https://cla.opensource.microsoft.com>.

When you submit a pull request, a CLA bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide
a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (e.g., status check, comment). Simply follow the instructions
provided by the bot. You will only need to do this once across all repos using our CLA.

This project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/).
For more information see the [Code of Conduct FAQ](https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/) or
contact [opencode@microsoft.com](mailto:opencode@microsoft.com) with any additional questions or comments.

## Trademarks

This project may contain trademarks or logos for projects, products, or services. Authorized use of Microsoft
trademarks or logos is subject to and must follow
[Microsoft's Trademark & Brand Guidelines](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/legal/intellectualproperty/trademarks/usage/general).
Use of Microsoft trademarks or logos in modified versions of this project must not cause confusion or imply Microsoft sponsorship.
Any use of third-party trademarks or logos are subject to those third-party's policies.

classes.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler.send
documentation: Sends the http request object to the next middleware or retries the request if necessary.

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler._get_current_options
documentation:
Returns the options to use for the request.Overries default options if
request options are passed.

Args:
    request (httpx.Request): The prepared request object

Returns:
    RetryHandlerOption: The options to used.

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler.should_retry
documentation:
Determines whether the request should be retried
Checks if the request method is in allowed methods
Checks if the response status code is in retryable status codes.

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler._is_method_retryable
documentation:
Checks if a given request should be retried upon, depending on
whether the HTTP method is in the set of allowed methods

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler._is_request_payload_buffered
documentation:
Checks if the request payload is buffered/rewindable.
Payloads with forward only streams will return false and have the responses
returned without any retry attempt.

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler.check_retry_valid
documentation: Check that the max retries limit has not been hit

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler.get_delay_time
documentation:
Get the time in seconds to delay between retry attempts.
Respects a retry-after header in the response if provided
If no retry-after response header, it defaults to exponential backoff

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler._get_delay_time_exp_backoff
documentation:
Get time in seconds to delay between retry attempts based on an exponential
backoff value.

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler._get_retry_after
documentation: Check if retry-after is specified in the response header and get the value

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.retry_handler.RetryHandler._parse_retry_after
documentation: Helper to parse Retry-After and get value in seconds.

Middleware that allows us to specify the retry policy for all requests
Retry configuration.
\:param int max_retries:

> Maximum number of retries to allow. Takes precedence over other counts.
> Set to `0` to fail on the first retry.

```
param iterable retry_on_status_codes

A set of integer HTTP status codes that we should force a retry on.
A retry is initiated if the request method is in allowed_methods
and the response status code is in RETRY STATUS CODES.

param float retry_backoff_factor

A backoff factor to apply between attempts after the second try
(most errors are resolved immediately by a second try without a
delay).
The request will sleep for:

source:15: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

{backoff factor} * (2 ** ({retry number} - 1))

source:16: (WARNING/2) Literal block ends without a blank line; unexpected unindent.

seconds. If the backoff_factor is 0.1, then  will sleep
for [0.0s, 0.2s, 0.4s, ...] between retries. It will never be longer
than .
By default, backoff is set to 0.5.

param int retry_time_limit

The maximum cumulative time in seconds that total retries should take.
The cumulative retry time and retry-after value for each request retry
will be evaluated against this value; if the cumulative retry time plus
the retry-after value is greater than the retry_time_limit, the failed
response will be immediately returned, else the request retry continues.
```

documentation:
Gets the parent_span from the request options and creates a new span.
If no parent_span is found, we try to get the current span.

Base class for middleware. Handles moving a Request to the next middleware in the pipeline.
If the current middleware is the last one in the pipeline, it makes a network request

classes.name: kiota_http.middleware.redirect_handler.RedirectHandler

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.redirect_handler.RedirectHandler.increment
documentation:
Increment the redirect attempts for this request.
Args
    response(httpx.Response): A httpx response object.
Returns:
    bool: Whether further redirect attempts are remaining.
    False if exhausted; True if more redirect attempts available.

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.redirect_handler.RedirectHandler.get_redirect_location
documentation:
Checks for redirect status code and gets redirect location.
Args:

> response(httpx.Response): The Response object

Returns:

: Union\[str, bool, None\]: Truthy redirect location string if we got a redirect status
  code and valid location. `None` if redirect status and no
  location. `False` if not a redirect status code.

methods.name: kiota_http.middleware.redirect_handler.RedirectHandler.is_https_redirect

Middlware that allows us to define the redirect policy for all requests

methods.name: kiota_http.kiota_client_factory.KiotaClientFactory.get_default_client
documentation:
Returns a native HTTP AsyncClient(httpx.AsyncClient) instance with default options

Returns:
    httpx.AsyncClient

methods.name: kiota_http.kiota_client_factory.KiotaClientFactory.create_with_default_middleware
documentation:
Constructs native HTTP AsyncClient(httpx.AsyncClient) instances configured with
a custom transport loaded with a default pipeline of middleware.

Args:
    options (Optional[Dict[str, RequestOption]]): The request options to use when
    instantiating default middleware. Defaults to Dict[str, RequestOption]=None.

Returns:
    httpx.AsycClient: An instance of the AsyncClient object

methods.name: kiota_http.kiota_client_factory.KiotaClientFactory.create_with_custom_middleware
documentation:
Constructs native HTTP AsyncClient(httpx.AsyncClient) instances configured with
a custom pipeline of middleware.

Args:
    middleware(List[BaseMiddleware]): Custom middleware list that will be used to create
    a middleware pipeline. The middleware should be arranged in the order in which they will
    modify the request.

methods.name: kiota_http.kiota_client_factory.KiotaClientFactory.get_default_middleware
documentation:
Helper method that returns a list of default middleware instantiated with
appropriate options

methods.name: kiota_http.kiota_client_factory.KiotaClientFactory.create_middleware_pipeline
documentation: Helper method that constructs a middleware_pipeline with the specified middleware

Returns:
    The parent span.

methods.name: kiota_http.httpx_request_adapter.HttpxRequestAdapter._start_local_tracing_span
documentation: Helper function to start a span locally with the parent context.

methods.name: kiota_http.httpx_request_adapter.HttpxRequestAdapter.send_async
documentation:
Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized response model.
Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation): the request info to execute.
    parsable_factory (ParsableFactory): the class of the response model
    to deserialize the response into.
    error_map (Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]): the error dict to use in
    case of a failed request.

Returns:
    ModelType: the deserialized response model.

methods.name: kiota_http.httpx_request_adapter.HttpxRequestAdapter.send_collection_async
documentation:
Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized response model collection.
Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation): the request info to execute.
    parsable_factory (ParsableFactory): the class of the response model
    to deserialize the response into.
    error_map (Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]): the error dict to use in
    case of a failed request.

Returns:
    ModelType: the deserialized response model collection.

methods.name: kiota_http.httpx_request_adapter.HttpxRequestAdapter.send_collection_of_primitive_async
documentation:
Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized response model collection.
Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation): the request info to execute.
    response_type (PrimitiveType): the class of the response model
    to deserialize the response into.
    error_map (Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]): the error dict to use in
    case of a failed request.

Returns:
    Optional[List[PrimitiveType]]: The deserialized primitive type collection.

methods.name: kiota_http.httpx_request_adapter.HttpxRequestAdapter.send_primitive_async
documentation:
Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized primitive response model.
Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation): the request info to execute.
    response_type (str): the class name of the response model to deserialize the
    response into.
    error_map (Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]): the error dict to use in case
    of a failed request.

Returns:
    Optional[ResponseType]: the deserialized primitive response model.

methods.name: kiota_http.httpx_request_adapter.HttpxRequestAdapter.send_no_response_content_async

Excutes the HTTP request specified by the given RequestInformation and returns the
deserialized primitive response model.
Args:
    request_info (RequestInformation):the request info to execute.
    error_map (Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]): the error dict to use in case

methods.name: kiota_http.httpx_request_adapter.HttpxRequestAdapter.enable_backing_store
documentation:
Enables the backing store proxies for the SerializationWriters and ParseNodes in use.
Args:
    backing_store_factory (Optional[BackingStoreFactory]): the backing store factory to use.

methods.name: kiota_http.httpx_request_adapter.HttpxRequestAdapter._error_class_not_in_error_mapping
documentation:
Helper function to check if the error class corresponding to a response status code
is not in the error mapping.

Args:
    error_map (Dict[str, type[ParsableFactory]]): The error mapping.
    status_code (int): The response status code.

Returns:
    bool: True if the error class is not in the error mapping, False otherwise.
